bids.reasoning: As this is the opening offer at the very beginning of the negotiation, I will start by proposing my most preferred values for all issues. This serves to maximize my potential utility and sets a strong initial anchor from which I can steadily make considered concessions in subsequent rounds.

The negotiation has just started (`t=0.02`), and the opponent's first offer represents my lowest possible utility, accompanied by a generic emotional appeal which I will treat as cheap talk. Since it is still extremely early, I will make a very small concession on my least important issue (`issueA`) by moving from valueF to valueE, maintaining my highest values on the more heavily weighted issues to keep my overall utility near its maximum.

The time fraction is still extremely early (t=0.04), allowing me to hold firm on my most important issues while demonstrating good faith. The opponent's message claims they are "putting everything on the table" and "meeting halfway", but their actual offer remains unchanged at the lowest possible utility for me (valueA across all issues). To encourage movement while protecting my high-weight outcomes, I will make a small concession on my lowest-priority issue, issueA, to valueD, while keeping my most critical issues (C and D) at their maximal values.

The negotiation is still in its very early stages (t=0.06) and the opponent's offers reflect a mostly unyielding position, with only a small concession on issueD. As issueD is my most heavily weighted priority (0.4 importance), I will hold firm on my top choice for it alongside my other higher-weight issues. I will demonstrate continued willingness to cooperate by making another small concession on issueA, my lowest-weighted issue.

The negotiation is still in its very early stages (t=0.08). The opponent has made a tiny concession on issueD while maintaining a strong stance on all other issues, despite their highly emotional messages claiming eagerness to collaborate. To continue signaling flexibility, I will make another concession on issueA, my lowest priority issue (weight 0.1), dropping it to valueB but holding perfectly firm on issues B, C, and D where my priorities are highest.

The opponent is employing a strategy of making extremely low offers paired with purely emotional appeals, which I will treat as cheap talk. At t=0.1, we are still very early in the negotiation. I will concede completely on my least important issue (issueA) to valueA in order to show flexibility and find common ground, while firmly holding my top utilities for my most important issues (issueB, issueC, issueD).

I have fully conceded on my least important issue (issueA) to demonstrate flexibility early in the negotiation. While the opponent's messages rely on emotional appeals, their actual offers remain extremely unfavorable and have barely moved. Now, with the time still early (t=0.12), I will begin making measured concessions on my second least important issue, issueB, while firmly protecting my highest-weight priorities (issueC and issueD).

The current time fraction is very low (t=0.14), so there is no rush to rapidly drop my utility. The opponent's messages consist primarily of emotional appeals and cheap talk, while their numeric bids show them demanding valueA heavily but slowly testing concessions on my most highly valued issues (issueC and issueD). To reciprocate their minor concessions while protecting my highest-weight issues, I will hold firm on issueC and issueD at valueF, maintain my full concession on issueA (my lowest weight), and make a small concession on issueB to valueD.

The opponent has consistently offered valueA for all issues but made minor concessions on issues B, C, and D in scattered offers. It is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.16), so I will maintain my top values for my most critical issues (issueD and issueC). Having already fully conceded on my least important issue (issueA), I will now make another small concession on issueB (from valueD to valueC) to signal flexibility while keeping my total utility high (0.824). I am treating the opponent's messages as pure emotional appeals and prioritizing the numerical signals.

The current time fraction is only 0.18, so we are still early in the negotiation. I will continue my pattern of gradual concessions on lower-weight issues while holding firm on my most important ones (issueC and issueD). Since I have fully conceded issueA (my lowest priority) to a value the opponent seems to strongly prefer, I will now lower my request on issueB to valueB, bringing my target utility to 0.79.

At time fraction 0.20, I have been systematically conceding on my lowest-priority issues (issueA and issueB) while the opponent has shown a strong preference for 'valueA' across the board, accompanied by emotional appeals but very small concessions. To demonstrate flexibility and test their priorities, I am going to completely yield on my two least important issues by offering 'valueA' for both issueA and issueB. In exchange, I will firmly maintain 'valueF' on issueC and issueD, which are critical to my overall utility, ensuring an offer outcome of 0.76 while attempting to solidify an agreement on half of the terms.

I have already conceded completely on my two least important issues (issueA and issueB) to demonstrate my willingness to find common ground. The opponent is making minor concessions across the board but continues to rely heavily on emotional appeals rather than strong moves toward my preferred values. To maintain momentum at this relatively early stage (t=0.22), I will now introduce a modest concession on issueC while holding firm on my most critical priority, issueD.

The opponent has made significant emotional appeals but has shown some real concessions on issueC (moving from valueA to valueD) while holding firm on issueD. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.24), I will maintain a fairly high utility target (~0.73) by insisting on valueF for my most critical issue (issueD) and valueE for my second most critical (issueC). I will concede significantly on issueA and issueB, exploring value combinations to find acceptable trade-offs for the opponent.

The opponent's messages rely entirely on repetitive emotional appeals and should be treated as cheap talk. They have made small concessions on issues B, C, and D over time, indicating some flexibility, while seeking their highest values (A). Because it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.26), I will make a measured concession by offering a mix of 'valueB' on my lower-weighted issues (A and B) and 'valueE' on issue C, while holding firm on my most critical priority, issueD, at 'valueF'.

I will maintain issueD at valueF since it carries the highest importance for me (0.4), and continue to offer valueE on issueC. Given the early stage of the negotiation (t=0.28), I want to stay around 0.75-0.80 total utility. I am offering a slightly different combination on the lower-priority issues (issueA and issueB) to probe the opponent's true preferences while disregarding their purely tactical emotional messages.

The opponent's messages are heavily based on emotional appeals without correspondingly significant concessions in their actual offers, signaling a tactic to win unreciprocated concessions. As we are still at t=0.30, I will discard the cheap talk and maintain a high utility, focusing my firmness on my highest-priority issue (issueD) while continuing to explore favorable combinations for the less critical issues (issueA and issueB) to probe their real priorities. I will target an offer that yields around 0.75 utility for myself.

I am targeting a utility around 0.74 as the negotiation is still relatively early (t=0.32). The opponent has been flooding the negotiation with emotional appeals and fake urgency, but their actual offers show a willingness to concede on my most important issue, issueD, reaching valueE most recently. To build on this, I will concede fully to their valueA on my least important issue (issueA) and lower my demand on issueB, while maintaining firmness on issueC and issueD where my weights are highest.

I am prioritizing my highest-weight issues (issueD and issueC) while making concessions on issueA and issueB where the opponent seems to need valueA. The opponent has recently explored giving valueE on issueD (t=0.32) and issueC (t=0.34). I will adapt to their willingness to yield on the highest-value issues by proposing a balanced configuration that requests valueE for issueD and valueF for issueC, while keeping my demands on issueA and issueB low to secure agreement moving towards the mid-game.

The current time is early-mid game (t=0.36). The opponent's message history uses emotional appeals while their bids bounce around, recently testing higher concessions on issueC (valueE) and issueD (valueE) at different times. I will capitalize on these signals by offering their favored values on my low-priority issues (issueA and issueB) while securing high utility (valueE/valueF) on my highest-priority issues (issueC and issueD). This delivers a compromise that aims to maintain a strong utility of ~0.70 for me while directly responding to their recent explorations.

I will maintain my high-utility demands on issueC and issueD, since they are my most heavily weighted issues (0.3 and 0.4, respectively). The opponent's frequent emotional messages are being treated as cheap talk, but their actual offers show a willingness to concede occasionally on issueC and issueD (such as offering valueE previously). I will offer a small concession on issueA and issueB to find a combination they might accept without severely reducing my own utility at this early-mid stage (t=0.38).

The current time is mid-game (0.4), so it's appropriate to continue making moderate concessions to signal flexibility and encourage the opponent, targeting an overall utility around 0.74–0.76. The opponent's historical offers indicate varying degrees of flexibility on issues C and D, so I will stabilize my requests on valueE for my top-priority issues (D and C) while trading off slightly more on issues A and B where my weights are lowest. I ignore the opponent's emotional appeals because they are pure negotiation tactics and offer no binding constraints.

The opponent is using heavy emotional appeals but has shown a willingness to concede on issueC (offering valueE this turn). I will accept their concession on issueC to valueE and maintain my demand for valueE on issueD, as these are my highest-weight issues. In return, I will concede closer to their signaled preferences on my lower-weight issues by offering valueB on issueA and valueC on issueB, targeting a utility of around 0.73 as we approach the mid-game.

The opponent's messages consist exclusively of emotional appeals, which I am disregarding in favor of their actual bidding behavior. Their recent offers show some flexibility on issueC and issueD (offering C=E at t=0.42 and D=D at t=0.40). Given we are approaching the midway point (t=0.44), I will offer a moderate concession on my lower-weighted issues (A and B) while keeping my highest-weighted issues (C and D) at a high utility to maintain an overall utility around 0.74.

The opponent is consistently aiming for valueA/valueB on issues A and B, which are my lowest-weight issues, while making gradual concessions on C and D (recently offering D's valueE). Since we are nearing the mid-game, I will offer a combination that secures my highest-priority values (C: valueE, D: valueF) while conceding heavily on my lower-weight issues (A: valueC, B: valueB) to find an acceptable compromise. I am ignoring the opponent's emotional appeals because they are purely cheap talk intended to apply psychological pressure.

The negotiation is nearing the halfway mark (t=0.48). The opponent's offers have shown some flexibility on issueD (offering valueE at t=0.46) and issueC (offering valueE earlier), while they clearly prioritize issueA and issueB. I will continue to capitalize on their willingness to concede on my most important issues (C and D) by matching some of their preferred values on my lower-weight issues (A and B), targeting a utility around 0.74 to continue narrowing the gap.

The opponent relies heavily on emotional appeals in their messages, which I am disregarding in favor of analyzing their actual bids. They have demonstrated a willingness to offer higher utility values for `issueC` (e.g., `valueE`) and `issueD` (up to `valueE`), showing they can concede on my highly valued issues. At t=0.5, I will propose a tradeoff that provides them with lower values on `issueA` and `issueB` (my least important issues), while holding firm on high-utility values for `issueC` and `issueD` to target an overall utility around 0.79.

I am ignoring the opponent's emotional appeals regarding their inability to stretch further, as their bidding history shows they are capable of offering concessions up to valueE on issues C and D. Since my highest weights are on issues C (0.3) and D (0.4), I will stubbornly protect utility in those areas while showing flexibility on issues A and B. I will offer the exact values they most recently proposed for issues A and B, in exchange for retaining high values on my most important issues, resulting in a solid utility of ~0.77.

The opponent's messages rely heavily on emotional appeals and repetitive pleading but provide no concrete information about their constraints, which I will treat as cheap talk. Their offers, however, indicate flexibility on issues C and D (they previously offered C=E and D=E). Since my preference heavily weights issues C and D, I will maintain high values (E and F) for these areas while conceding slightly more on issues A and B, which are my lowest priorities but seem to matter significantly to the opponent. I'm targeting a utility around 0.76 to demonstrate continuing flexibility just past the midpoint.

I am targeting a utility around 0.74, a steady concession from my previous offer as we cross the halfway point (t=0.56). The opponent continues to use repetitive emotional appeals while scattering their concessions across different issues. To find common ground without sacrificing my core interests, I will propose top-range values (E) on my highest-weighted issues (C and D, which they have previously signaled willingness to accept in separate offers) while offering moderate values on A and B.

The opponent's emotional messages are meant to pressure me into conceding, but their latest offer (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueE, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueD) shows they are willing to yield significantly on issueB and moderately on issueD. Since issueA is my lowest priority (weight 0.1), I will agree to their preferred valueA on issueA and adopt their concession to valueE on issueB. This allows me to maintain firm targets of valueE on issueC and issueD, aiming for a favorable agreement well above my reservation value as we head into the mid-late stages of the negotiation.

The opponent's emotional appeals are discarded as cheap talk. Seeing their history, they frequently request high values (A/B/C) on `issueA` and `issueB`, while demonstrating some flexibility to offer values D and E on `issueC` and `issueD`. Since my weights are highest on `issueC` and `issueD`, I will concede further on the first two issues where my weights are low, targeting `valueC` and `valueB` respectively, in order to secure top-tier values for my most critical issues, keeping my overall utility around 0.77.

The opponent has made a significant concession on my highest-priority issue (issueD), offering valueF. I want to lock in issueD at valueF while securing good utility on issueC. To show reciprocal flexibility, I will concede fully on issueB (offering valueA, which they frequently requested in earlier rounds) and offer valueC on issueA, bringing my target utility around 0.744. The opponent's messages are purely emotional appeals ("heartbroken," "goodwill") and contain no factual constraints to incorporate.

At t=0.64, I am observing that the opponent recently proposed `valueF` for my most important issue (issueD) and just offered `valueB` for issueA and `valueD` for issueB. I will align entirely with their last offer on my two lowest-weight issues, proposing `valueB` for issueA and `valueD` for issueB, while continuing to hold firm on issueC (`valueE`) and demanding `valueF` on issueD. This strategy captures high utility (0.82) on my top issues while demonstrating significant concessions on the issues the opponent clearly prioritizes.

The opponent's most recent offer provided a substantial concession on issue C (valueF) while holding issue D at valueE. Since they previously offered valueF for issue D (at t=0.62), I will accept their exact terms for issues A, B, and C, and ask for a slight upgrade back to valueF on issue D. The opponent's messages rely entirely on emotional appeals and non-specific sacrifices, indicating we are close to a mutually acceptable tradeoff without rigid constraints.

The opponent's messages are highly repetitive emotional appeals, claiming significant sacrifice, but their offers show they highly value issueA and issueB (often demanding valueA or valueB). Since my highest priorities are issueC and issueD, I will maintain firm on those issues (`valueF`) while offering concessions on my less critical issues (`issueA` and `issueB`), proposing `valueB` for both. This yields over 0.80 utility for me while potentially bringing us closer to a deal as we approach the final third of our time.

The negotiation is at t=0.7 and the opponent has consistently used emotional appeals which I am treating as cheap talk. I will focus entirely on their bidding behavior. Recently, they have shown a willingness to accept `valueC` for `issueA` and `issueB`, `valueF` for `issueC`, and `valueE` for `issueD` across their t=0.66 to t=0.70 bids. I will combine these previously offered values to construct a bid that remains highly favorable for me (targeting ~0.79 utility) while matching terms the opponent has already signaled they can accept.

The opponent's emotional messages are treated as cheap talk, but their recent offers reveal flexibility. They recently offered valueF on issueC and valueE on issueD (at t=0.66), and valueD on issueB (at t=0.72). I will combine these revealed preferences to offer valueB for issueA, valueD for issueB, valueF for issueC, and valueE for issueD, maintaining a high utility of ~0.81 for myself while attempting to find a mutually acceptable package.

The opponent's most recent offer surprisingly included valueF for issueD, our highest-priority issue, which raises our utility significantly despite their emotional messages. To capitalize on this, I will lock in valueF for issueD and maintain strong utility on issueC. In exchange, I will concede on our lowest-priority issues by offering valueA for issueA and valueD for issueB, both of which the opponent has frequently targeted in their past offers.
